FRA.optaNote: resolved to 0 by elimination Jul 14; Opta's Jul 12 article still shows 34.0

outcomes.note: SF1 resolved: Spain 2-0 France (Jul 14, Arlington; Oyarzabal 22' pen, Porro 58'). SF2 England v Argentina was LIVE (0-0, first half) at pass time - left null; Run 4 fills it and grades the semis scorecards.

carriedForwardFromRun2: All Run 1-2 verified facts stand: Opta Jul 12 figures (now partially resolved by reality), Silver paywall note, penalty superlative softening, five named banks, Miami Herald ~$90M, La Nacion $260M/$57M attribution, Tofoni ~3h videoconference interview (witness, not charged), AFA pushback quote, no US charges, FBI declined comment.
newSinceRun2: Spain 2-0 France (Jul 14): Oyarzabal 22' penalty (Digne foul on Lamine Yamal - hotly disputed, VAR did not overturn), Pedro Porro 58'. Referee Ivan Barton (El Salvador). Spain xG ~1.63 v ~0.31. France eliminated; Spain in the Jul 19 final.

The SF1 penalty produced its own officiating firestorm with Argentina uninvolved: Deschamps questioned whether the referee 'had the level' for a semifinal; Collina defended the official; ex-PGMOL chief Keith Hackett called the penalty correct. Added as timeline node + H4 forPoint: scrutiny is tournament-wide, not Argentina-specific.

Market flipped to Spain as clear favorite: DraftKings ESP -156 / ENG +300 / ARG +400; FanDuel -150/+280/+370; Kalshi 57.6/22.9/19.8. models.ts BOOKMAKERS refreshed asOf 2026-07-15; author ranges re-synthesized (ESP .52-.60, ENG .21-.26, ARG .17-.23, FRA 0).

Opta NOT refreshed post-SF1 at pass time (re-verified by fetch Jul 15); elimination-resolved zeros applied for France with comments; other teams keep last published figures flagged as pre-SF1. Opta's 57.7% France-to-final lean resolved to the 42.3% branch.

England v Argentina live at pass time (0-0 first half, Atlanta). England: Rice started despite illness doubt; three changes (Rogers, Spence, R. James); Henderson out (arm/wrist reported inconsistently - kept vague). Result deliberately left null.

Financial thread: no material movement; coverage is syndication of known facts. One attributed nugget added: Tapia (processed in the separate Argentine case) reportedly needed judicial authorization to travel to the US with the squad.
Hypothesis bands unchanged (H1 2-8, H2 70-90/45-65, H3 35-55, H4 30-50).
